note: Transcribed from a screenshot of charuco_calibration.py output for the RIGHT camera (cam3). image_width/image_height confirmed as 640x480 via v4l2-ctl --list-formats-ext output (cx=316.7, cy=240.7 are consistent with a 640x480 frame center). If charuco_calibration.py was actually run at a DIFFERENT supported resolution (e.g. 1280x720, 1920x1080), this K matrix and these image dimensions are mismatched and must be re-derived -- confirm against what CAMERA_INDEX/resolution the script actually opened during calibration.
